subject: Cecil
original_context_text: " Mr. Otis was a good deal distressed at Lord Canterville's refusal, and begged [[him]] to reconsider his decision, but the good-natured peer was quite firm, and finally induced the Minister to allow his daughter to retain the present the ghost had given her, and when, in the spring of 1890, the young Duchess of Cheshire was presented at the Queen's first drawing-room on the occasion of her marriage, her jewels were the universal theme of admiration.
